channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A chatbot entirely powered by humans, not artificial intelligence? This Chilean community shows why
item.description: About 50 members of a community outside Chile’s capital spent Saturday trying their best to power an entirely human-operated chatbot that could answer questions and make silly pictures on command, in a message to highlight the environmental toll and water usage of artificial intelligence data center
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Why a Tennessee proposal to ban sports betting on campus is too little, too late
item.description: In the months after a 2018 Supreme Court decision opened the door for states to legalize sports betting within their borders, giddy lawmakers across the country couldn’t move quickly enough.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok’s odd behavior was caused by a tech issue—but users’ response indicates the app has a trust problem
item.description: Anyone wanting to get their TikTok fix in the United States recently had a rough time. The app went haywire, kicking off early on the morning of January 25, due to a power outage at a key data center that knocked out services nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Resilience is essential as demands on government networks multiply
item.description: Agencies can’t predict the next attack, new mandates or the next cloud outage. But they can control the strength of their network foundation.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: GSA ends U.S. participation in ‘open government’ initiative
item.description: GSA argued the initiative was ineffective. Civil society experts say it’s another example of the Trump admin turning its back on openness and transparency.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal CIO outlines ‘one government’ blueprint for overhauling digital services
item.description: Federal Chief Information Officer Greg Barbaccia said his top priority is to get the federal government to “obsess over delivering tangible outcomes.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Startup Amutable plotting Linux security overhaul to counter hacking threats
item.description: If there’s one thing guaranteed to grab attention in the computer security world, it’s announcing yourself without fully explaining what it is you plan to do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: This whole AI thing is simpler than you think
item.description: At least three-quarters of the speaking invitations I get these days are about AI. But lately, they’re for different reasons. Companies used to bring me in under the assumption that artificial intelligence was going to change everything.
item.dc:creator: Douglas Rushkoff, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s upbeat forecast clouded by fears about rising costs
item.description: Apple Inc. delivered record quarterly sales and a better-than-anticipated forecast for the current period, even as the company warned that rising component costs are threatening to squeeze margins.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco (Nasdaq:CSCO) is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Cisco is focusing on strategic AI initiatives and partnerships across various regions to build and power AI data centers and ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon in talks to invest up to $50 billion in OpenAI, WSJ says
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. is in talks to invest as much as $50 billion in OpenAI, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday, a deal that would accelerate ties between the world's largest cloud-computing provider and the leading builder of AI tools.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: New PDF compression filter will save space, need software updates
item.description: Brotli is one of the most widely used but least-known compression formats ever devised, long incorporated into all major browsers and web content delivery networks (CDNs).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Humanoid robot makes architectural history by designing a building
item.description: Ai-Da Robot became the first humanoid robot to design a building with a modular Space Pod concept for Moon and Mars bases, featured at the Utzon Center in Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From Meta to Samsung, global tech unleashes spending to chase AI
item.description: The world's largest tech firms show no signs of easing up on AI spending, a record wave that's propelling hardware providers like Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Commerce Department plans national AI center in San Francisco
item.description: The Trump administration is looking to increase the country’s edge in AI and defense, industries that are largely based in California.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Thousands of iPhone apps expose data inside Apple App Store
item.description: Researchers found Apple iOS app security flaws that expose user data and payment systems despite positive App Store reviews, according to Cybernews.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why your next microservices should be streaming SQL-driven
item.description: “It is tempting, if the only tool you have is a hammer, to treat everything as if it were a nail.” — Abraham Maslow, 1966 Microservices provide many benefits for building business services, such as independent tools, technologies, languages, release cycles, and full control over your dependencies.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A sweeping vision for the Navy is colliding with harsh shipbuilding realities
item.description: "Think of U.S. shipbuilding as an enterprise, facing enterprise-wide challenges," Seamus Daniels said. "This is a systemic problem with many interwoven issues.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Crooks are hijacking and reselling AI infrastructure: Report
item.description: For years, CSOs have worried about their IT infrastructure being used for unauthorized cryptomining. Now, say researchers, they’d better start worrying about crooks hijacking and reselling access to exposed corporate AI infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google’s LiteRT adds advanced hardware acceleration
item.description: LiteRT, Google’s “modern” on-device inference framework evolved from TensorFlow Lite (TFLite), has introduced advanced acceleration capabilities, based on a ”next-generation GPU engine” called ML Drift.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: WhatsApp gets new security features
item.description: The popular messaging app WhatsApp got a range of new security features Tuesday with the launch of “Strict Account Settings.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Three reasons that should drive your endpoint management strategy in 2026
item.description: 2026 will be the year many government IT teams either implement their first unified endpoint management (UEM) strategy or replace outdated ones entirely.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Tiny autonomous robots can now swim on their own
item.description: Scientists create smallest programmable robots ever – smaller than salt grains, these breakthrough microscopic machines could revolutionize medicine and healthcare.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta-Corning fiber deal signals a new bottleneck in AI infrastructure
item.description: Meta has announced a $6 billion, multi-year fiber supply agreement with Corning, highlighting how AI infrastructure constraints are shifting beyond compute and into the physical network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Teradata unveils enterprise AgentStack to push AI agents into production
item.description: Teradata has expanded the agent-building capabilities it launched last year into a full-blown toolkit, which it says will help enterprises address the challenge of moving AI agents beyond pilots into production-grade deployments.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: To fix federal HR, Washington must stop rewarding failure
item.description: This is the "maintenance trap" in action: Agencies spend so much time and money keeping broken systems running that they can't afford to replace them.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network
